title: Lasso as Feature Selection
concept: By driving unimportant feature weights to exactly zero, Lasso automatically selects the most critical features.

exercises.prompt: Recap: In Lasso Regression (L1), what happens to less important feature weights?

options.text: They are driven to exactly zero
They are doubled to increase their influence
They are set to the average weight of all features

exercises.explain: Exactly! Lasso drives unimportant weights to exactly zero, removing them from the model.

cards.title: Finding the needle
cards.body: Imagine you're trying to predict house prices using 100 features, including the number of bathrooms and the color of the owner's front door. Storing and calculating all 100 features is slow and wasteful.

cards.title: Automatic selection
cards.body: Because Lasso drives unimportant weights to exactly zero, it acts as an automatic feature selector, keeping only the variables that actually matter for predictions.

cards.title: Cleaning the clutter
cards.body: Think of Lasso as a Marie Kondo tidying sweep. Instead of just organizing your clutter (Ridge), Lasso asks: 'Does this feature spark joy?' If not, it throws it away entirely (sets weight to zero).

cards.title: Medical diagnosis
cards.body: To diagnose a rare disease, you might collect 1,000 health measurements. Lasso can narrow this down to a handful of key indicators (like blood pressure and age), ignoring the other 995 features.

cards.title: Not always the best selection
cards.body: A common myth is that Lasso always picks the absolute best features. If two features are highly correlated (like outdoor temperature in Celsius and Fahrenheit), Lasso will randomly pick one and discard the other!

left.text:
Test features one by one.
Time-consuming and slow.
Prone to human bias.

right.text:
Learns weights automatically.
Sets useless weights to zero.
Fast, mathematical, objective.

exercises.prompt: Why is Lasso Regression considered a tool for feature selection?

options.text: It eliminates features by setting their weights to exactly zero
It lets you manually click on which features you want to keep
It generates new synthetic features automatically

exercises.explain: By forcing unimportant weights to zero, Lasso automatically filters out useless features.

exercises.prompt: True or false: If two features are highly correlated, Lasso will keep both and share the weight equally.
exercises.explain: False! When features are highly correlated, Lasso tends to randomly pick one and discard the other.

exercises.sentence: When a model has many weights set to exactly zero, we call it a ___ model.
exercises.explain: A sparse model has mostly zero weights, meaning it uses only a subset of features.

exercises.prompt: Which of the following is an example of Lasso performing feature selection?

options.text: A house-price model setting the weight of 'lawn color' to 0
A model calculating the average age of buyers
A model increasing all weights by 10%

exercises.explain: Setting the weight of an unimportant feature (like lawn color) to zero removes it from predictions.

exercises.prompt: You are analyzing credit card fraud with 500 transaction features. Many features are redundant (like transaction city and transaction zip code). What will Lasso do?

options.text: It will pick one of the redundant features and set the other's weight to zero.
It will assign equal weights to both features to keep them both.
It will fail because redundant features cause division by zero.

exercises.explain: Spot on! Lasso will select one of the correlated features and discard the other, helping simplify the model but potentially losing some context. Next: let's compare L1 and L2 head-to-head!
